differential_diagnoses: Pyogenic arthritis, pyoderma gangrenosum, and acne (PAPA) syndrome
next_steps: Perform skin biopsy of the ulcer edge for histopathology and tissue culture to rule out infection
Consult dermatology for evaluation of pathergy phenomenon
Initiate systemic corticosteroids (e.g., prednisone or methylprednisolone) if infection is ruled out
Consult gastroenterology for colonoscopy and calprotectin levels to evaluate for underlying IBD/Crohn's disease
Discontinue unnecessary broad-spectrum antibiotics to avoid exacerbating gut dysbiosis
Perform serum inflammatory workup including CRP, ESR, and PASH/PAPA genetic panel

differential_diagnoses: PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Suppurative Hidradenitis)
Systemic autoinflammatory disorder (e.g., Adult-onset Still's disease or related Periodic Fever Syndrome)
next_steps: Perform diagnostic biopsy of the liver lesions to evaluate for sterile neutrophilic inflammation or granulomas
Perform skin biopsy of the ulcer edge with tissue culture (aerobic, anaerobic, fungal, mycobacterial) and histopathology to confirm neutrophilic dermatosis
Consult rheumatology for evaluation of systemic autoinflammatory markers including serum ferritin, soluble IL-2 receptor, and cytokine profile
Perform comprehensive GI evaluation including colonoscopy and fecal calprotectin to assess for occult IBD
Obtain autoimmune workup (ANA, ANCA, anti-phospholipid antibodies, complement levels)
Consult cardiology for formal evaluation of right heart strain and potential cardiac involvement (e.g., cardiac MRI or right heart catheterization)
Initiate pulsed systemic corticosteroids (if infection is excluded by preliminary cultures) to manage severe systemic neutrophilic inflammation
